description: Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) status indicated a duplicate for IPv6 anycast gateway configuration. As a workaround, configure an active gateway IPv6 address and SVI IPv6 address, as this can prevent DAD issues. Issuing the commands interface shut followed by interface no-shut can help in resolving the issue.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for ARP?

Feature Caveat: ARP - Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) status indicated a duplicate for IPv6 anycast gateway configuration. As a workaround, configure an active gateway IPv6 address and SVI IPv6 address, as this can prevent DAD issues. Issuing the commands interface shut followed by interface no-shut can help in resolving the issue.

description: After an issue with duplicate IPs on two VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) is resolved, the kernel will have the ARP entry, but the output of the show arp and show evpn mac-ip commands do not show that IP. As a workaround, flap the physical interface.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what limitation is mentioned for ARP?

Feature Caveat: ARP - After an issue with duplicate IPs on two VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) is resolved, the kernel will have the ARP entry, but the output of the show arp and show evpn mac-ip commands do not show that IP. As a workaround, flap the physical interface.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp 1.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: When a large number of host-routes (fully qualified routes) ipv4 routes with /32 or ipv6 routes with /128 are installed on a system, with a matching directly connected host, some network events may result in traffic loss for those routes. Scenario: This issue is most oftenseen when the switch reboots, however it may be present when it initially is brought up. Link-flaps could also trigger this issue. Workaround: Clear the ARP cache.

description: Symptom: An ARP Cache entry with inactive IP addresses on the network may stay in the neighbor database for a long duration. Scenario: This issue can occur when the MAC refresh/relearn process occurs for the MACaddress mapped to inactive IP addresses. Workaround: Execute the command clear arp-cache then configure a reduced ARP ageout.
symptom: An ARP Cache entry with inactive IP addresses on the network may stay in the neighbor database for a long duration.

description: Symptom: The switch is not able to match the route with AS-path using a regular expression (^$). Scenario: In a multifabric scenario, the switch will advertise to the edge device and will also get a route from the other fabric. If the the edge device should not allow one fabric route to go to another fabric then manipulate the route in the border VTEP directly. Workaround: Match using the communities in the border VTEP and advertise to OSPF towards the edge.

description: Symptom: VSX sync is no longer syncing after a configuring BGP to redistribute to OSPF with a route map. Scenario: This issue can occur on a pair of switches in a VSX configuration that are syncing for BGP when a user configures redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map on the primary switch. VSX sync will not sync the redistribution configuration or any other new configuration. Workaround: Disable BGP syncing or remove the redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map.

description: Symptom: Some hosts may experience connectivity issues. Scenario: In an EVPN environment with ARP suppression enabled, random hosts could experience connectivity issues due to missing the associated ARP entry. Workaround: Temporarily add andremove a static ARP entry using the following commands Copy interface vlan <vlan_id>

description: Symptom: An ARP Cache entry with inactive IP addresses on the network may stay in the neighbor database for a long duration. Scenario: This issue can occur when the MAC refresh/relearn process occurs for the MACaddress mapped to inactive IP addresses. Workaround: Execute the command clear arp-cache then configure a reduced ARP ageout.

description: Symptom: The switch is not able to match the route with AS-path using a regular expression (^$). Scenario: In a multifabric scenario, the switch will advertise to the edge device and will also get a route from the other fabric. If the the edge device should not allow one fabric route to go to another fabric then manipulate the route in the border VTEP directly. Workaround: Match using the communities in the border VTEP and advertise to OSPF towards the edge.

description: Symptom: Growth in the boot.log file file size may lead to an increase in RAM utilization. Scenario: This issue can be triggered by a high rate of unauthorized or wrong requests to the REST HTTPS server, which could occur during malicious attacks or vulnerability tool tests.

description: Symptom: VSX sync is no longer syncing after a configuring BGP to redistribute to OSPF with a route map. Scenario: This issue can occur on a pair of switches in a VSX configuration that are syncing for BGP when a user configures redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map on the primary switch. VSX sync will not sync the redistribution configuration or any other new configuration. Workaround: Disable BGP syncing or remove the redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map.

workaround: Disable BGP syncing or remove the redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map.

description: Symptom: Some hosts may experience connectivity issues. Scenario: In an EVPN environment with ARP suppression enabled, random hosts could experience connectivity issues due to missing the associated ARP entry. Workaround: Temporarily add andremove a static ARP entry using the following commands Copy interface vlan <vlan_id>
symptom: Some hosts may experience connectivity issues.
scenario: In an EVPN environment with ARP suppression enabled, random hosts could experience connectivity issues due to missing the associated ARP entry
